title: May AI Take Your Order? The Rise and Challenges of Drive-Thru Chatbots
subtitle: Fast-food chains are using artificial intelligence to speed up service, but customers are not always happy with the results.

summary.en: Fast-food companies have started using AI chatbots to take orders at drive-thru windows. These systems are designed to improve efficiency and help staff focus on other tasks. However, many customers prefer talking to human workers instead of machines. Some people have even tried to trick the technology by making strange requests. As a result, some major chains are now rethinking their plans for these digital systems.
summary.zh: 速食業者已開始使用人工智慧聊天機器人在得來速窗口處理訂單。這些系統旨在提升效率並協助員工專注於其他任務。然而，許多顧客仍偏好與真人對話而非機器。有些人甚至試圖透過提出奇怪的要求來戲弄這項技術。因此，一些大型連鎖店目前正在重新思考其數位系統的相關計畫。

paragraphs.en: In recent years, the way we order food at fast-food restaurants has begun to change. Instead of speaking to a person, customers at many drive-thrus are now greeted by artificial intelligence (AI) chatbots. These computer programs are designed to listen to your order, understand your requests, and send the information to the kitchen. While companies hope this technology will make service faster and more accurate, the transition has been far from smooth.
paragraphs.zh: 近年來，我們在速食餐廳點餐的方式已開始轉變。在許多得來速（drive-thru）窗口，顧客不再是與真人對話，而是由人工智慧聊天機器人接待。這些電腦程式旨在聆聽您的訂單、理解您的需求，並將資訊傳送至廚房。雖然企業希望這項技術能讓服務更快速、更精確，但轉型過程卻遠非順遂。

paragraphs.en: The trend began in 2021 when McDonald’s started testing voice-ordering technology at several locations in Chicago. The company had purchased a startup called Apprente to help build these systems and later worked with IBM to expand the project. Soon after, other major chains followed this path. According to The Verge, companies like Checkers and Rally’s teamed up with an AI provider called Presto in 2022 to put chatbots in all their corporate-owned drive-thrus. The goal was to sell more food and improve the accuracy of orders. By 2023, Wendy’s launched its own system called “FreshAI” in Ohio, which was trained to understand specific menu names like “Frosty.” Taco Bell also joined the movement, announcing plans to bring voice AI to hundreds of locations.
paragraphs.zh: 這股趨勢始於二〇二一年，當時麥當勞（McDonald’s）開始在芝加哥的幾家分店測試語音點餐技術。該公司收購了一家名為 Apprente 的新創公司來協助建立這些系統，隨後並與 IBM 合作擴大該計畫。不久之後，其他大型連鎖店也跟進。根據科技新聞網站「邊緣」（The Verge）報導，像 Checkers 和 Rally’s 等公司在二〇二二年與人工智慧供應商 Presto 合作，將聊天機器人導入所有企業直營的得來速。其目標是銷售更多餐點並提升訂單準確度。到了二〇二三年，溫蒂漢堡（Wendy’s）在俄亥俄州推出了名為「FreshAI」的自家系統，該系統經過訓練，能理解如「冰霜奶昔」（Frosty）等特定的菜單名稱。塔可鐘（Taco Bell）也加入這波浪潮，宣布計畫將語音人工智慧導入數百家分店。

paragraphs.en: Fast-food chains often describe these systems as a way to help their employees. By letting a computer handle the ordering process, companies say they can reduce the workload for staff. This allows workers to focus on other important tasks, such as preparing food or cleaning the restaurant. Taco Bell, for example, told the public that the technology would help cut down on long wait times for customers. Other popular chains, including Panera Bread, White Castle, and Popeyes, have also started testing similar systems to see if they can improve their daily operations.
paragraphs.zh: 速食連鎖店常將這些系統描述為協助員工的方式。企業表示，透過讓電腦處理點餐流程，可以減輕員工的工作負擔。這讓員工能專注於其他重要任務，例如準備餐點或清潔餐廳。舉例來說，塔可鐘曾向大眾表示，這項技術有助於縮短顧客的等待時間。其他知名連鎖店，包括 Panera Bread、White Castle 和大力水手炸雞（Popeyes），也已開始測試類似系統，以評估是否能改善日常營運。

paragraphs.en: However, the experience for the customer has been mixed. Many people find it difficult to talk to a machine that might not understand them perfectly. Some customers have even expressed their frustration on social media. According to The Verge, some people have tried to trick the AI by making strange orders, such as asking for 18,000 cups of water, or by speaking in different languages just to force the system to connect them to a human worker. A survey conducted by YouGov in early 2025 showed that 55 percent of Americans would prefer a human to take their order. Only 4 percent of those surveyed said they would rather use an AI chatbot.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，顧客的體驗卻好壞參半。許多人發現與可能無法完全理解自己的機器對話相當困難。一些顧客甚至在社群媒體上表達了挫折感。根據「邊緣」報導，有些人試圖透過下達奇怪的訂單來戲弄人工智慧，例如要求一萬八千杯水，或是使用不同語言對話，只為了強迫系統將他們轉接給真人服務人員。由輿觀調查公司（YouGov）在二〇二五年初進行的一項調查顯示，百分之五十五的美國人更傾向由真人處理點餐。在受訪者中，僅有百分之四的人表示願意使用人工智慧聊天機器人。

paragraphs.en: This negative feedback is starting to change how companies view the technology. Because of the lukewarm response from the public, some businesses are reconsidering their strategies. For instance, McDonald’s ended its partnership with IBM in 2024. Similarly, Taco Bell has begun to reevaluate its use of AI drive-thrus after seeing the frustration expressed by customers online. It is clear that while the technology is meant to make things easier, it has created new problems for both the companies and the people they serve.
paragraphs.zh: 這些負面回饋正開始改變企業對這項技術的看法。由於大眾反應冷淡，一些企業正在重新評估其策略。例如，麥當勞在二〇二四年終止了與 IBM 的合作關係。同樣地，塔可鐘在目睹顧客在網路上表達的挫折感後，也開始重新評估其對人工智慧得來速的使用。顯而易見的是，雖然這項技術旨在簡化流程，但它卻為企業及其服務的對象帶來了新的問題。

paragraphs.en: Beyond customer satisfaction, there are other issues to consider. The Verge noted that the credibility of these technology companies is also a concern. For example, the Securities and Exchange Commission—the government agency that protects investors—has charged Presto with issues related to its business. This adds another layer of difficulty for chains that are trying to rely on these outside tech partners to run their drive-thrus.
paragraphs.zh: 除了顧客滿意度之外，還有其他問題需要考量。「邊緣」指出，這些科技公司的信譽也是一大隱憂。例如，負責保護投資人的政府機構「證券交易委員會」（Securities and Exchange Commission），已對 Presto 提出與其業務相關的指控。這對於試圖依賴這些外部科技合作夥伴來營運得來速的連鎖店而言，又增添了一層困難。

paragraphs.en: As we look toward the future, it remains uncertain how common these AI chatbots will become. While the technology is designed to be efficient, it cannot yet replace the human touch that many customers expect. For now, the fast-food industry is learning that technology is not always the perfect solution for every problem. Whether these systems will eventually improve enough to win over the public or disappear from drive-thrus entirely is a question that many companies are still trying to answer.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，這些人工智慧聊天機器人將會普及到什麼程度仍是未知數。雖然這項技術旨在提高效率，但它目前還無法取代許多顧客所期待的人性化互動。就目前而言，速食產業正在學習到，技術並不總是解決所有問題的完美方案。這些系統最終是否能改善到足以贏得大眾青睞，還是會完全從得來速消失，這仍是許多企業仍在努力尋找答案的問題。

keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（平滑的），這裡指過程順利。文中：the transition has been far from smooth.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To reduce the size, amount, or duration of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞與介系詞組成，意思非字面直譯。文中：Taco Bell, for example, told the public that the technology would help cut down on long wait times for customers.
字面意思為「微溫的」，這裡形容大眾對科技的反應冷淡。文中：Because of the lukewarm response from the public, some businesses are reconsidering their strategies.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To persuade someone to support you or agree with you.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見的片語，指成功爭取到他人的認同。文中：Whether these systems will eventually improve enough to win over the public or disappear from drive-thrus entirely is a question that many companies are still trying to answer.

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: Chatbots at the drive-thru are just the beginning

quiz.question: According to the article, what was a specific reason given by Taco Bell for adopting voice AI technology?

options.A: To increase the number of menu items available to customers
options.B: To decrease the amount of time customers spend waiting for their orders
options.C: To replace all human staff members with automated systems
options.D: To allow employees to work from home instead of at the restaurant

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Taco Bell 向大眾表示該技術將有助於減少顧客的等待時間（cut down on long wait times）。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章提到 AI 是為了幫助員工，而非完全取代；選項 D 文中完全未提及員工遠端工作的可能性。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between customer feedback and the future of AI in fast-food drive-thrus?

options.A: Companies are ignoring customer complaints to focus on long-term profits
options.B: The negative response has led companies to pause or reconsider their AI implementation plans
options.C: Customers have successfully forced all major chains to remove AI from their restaurants
options.D: AI technology is expected to become more popular once customers get used to it

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第五段，由於公眾反應冷淡（lukewarm response），McDonald's 結束了與 IBM 的合作，Taco Bell 也開始重新評估其 AI 得來速的使用。這顯示企業正因負面回饋而調整策略。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 過於誇大，文中僅提到重新評估，並非全面移除；選項 D 屬於臆測，文章結尾表示未來仍充滿不確定性。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'lukewarm' mean as it is used to describe the public's response?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第五段中，作者使用 'lukewarm' 來描述公眾對 AI 的反應，對照前後文提到顧客感到挫折（frustration）以及僅有 4% 的人偏好使用 AI，可知這裡是指「不熱衷的、反應冷淡的」。選項 B 語氣過重；選項 C 是描述顧客對系統的感受，而非對整體技術的反應；選項 D 則與 'lukewarm' 的字面與隱喻義完全相反。

quiz.question: Which statement best summarizes the central message of the article?

options.A: AI technology has successfully revolutionized the fast-food industry by making service perfectly accurate
options.B: Fast-food companies are moving away from human workers entirely to save money on labor costs
options.C: The implementation of AI in drive-thrus is a complex challenge that has faced significant public resistance
options.D: The Securities and Exchange Commission is the main reason why AI drive-thrus are failing to succeed

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文旨在探討 AI 進入得來速的過程，從最初的目標到面臨的技術與顧客滿意度挑戰，最後總結其未來的不確定性。選項 A 與文中提到的「transition has been far from smooth」矛盾；選項 B 非文章主旨；選項 D 僅是文中提到的一個次要挑戰，並非導致失敗的主要原因。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
